   173→        a_known1 = a.known_mask & a.value_mask
   174→        b_known1 = b.known_mask & b.value_mask
   175→        result_known1 = a_known1 | b_known1
   176→
   177→        a_known0 = a.known_mask & ~a.value_mask
   178→        b_known0 = b.known_mask & ~b.value_mask
   179→        result_known0 = a_known0 & b_known0
   180→
   181→        return TriValue(result_known1, result_known0 | result_known1, a.width)
   182→
   183→    @staticmethod
   184→    def xor2(a: 'TriValue', b: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   185→        both_known = a.known_mask & b.known_mask
   186→        return TriValue((a.value_mask ^ b.value_mask) & both_known, both_known, a.width)
   187→
   188→    @staticmethod
   189→    def mux(sel: 'TriValue', a: 'TriValue', b: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   190→        if sel.known_mask & 1:
   191→            return a.copy() if sel.value_mask & 1 else b.copy()
   192→
   193→        both_known = a.known_mask & b.known_mask
   194→        same_value = both_known & ~(a.value_mask ^ b.value_mask)
   195→        return TriValue((a.value_mask | b.value_mask) & same_value, same_value, a.width)
   196→
   197→    @staticmethod
   198→    def eq(a: 'TriValue', b: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   199→        mask = (1 << a.width) - 1
   200→        both_known = a.known_mask & b.known_mask
   201→
   202→        diff_bits = both_known & (a.value_mask ^ b.value_mask)
   203→        if diff_bits:
   204→            return TriValue(0, 1, 1)
   205→
   206→        if both_known == mask and not (a.value_mask ^ b.value_mask):
   207→            return TriValue(1, 1, 1)
   208→
   209→        return TriValue(0, 0, 1)
   210→
   211→    @staticmethod
   212→    def reduce_and(a: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   213→        mask = (1 << a.width) - 1
   214→        if a.known_mask & ~a.value_mask:
   215→            return TriValue(0, 1, 1)
   216→        if a.known_mask == mask and (a.value_mask & mask) == mask:
   217→            return TriValue(1, 1, 1)
   218→        return TriValue(0, 0, 1)
   219→
   220→    @staticmethod
   221→    def reduce_or(a: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   222→        mask = (1 << a.width) - 1
   223→        if a.known_mask & a.value_mask:
   224→            return TriValue(1, 1, 1)
   225→        if a.known_mask == mask and (a.value_mask & mask) == 0:
   226→            return TriValue(0, 1, 1)
   227→        return TriValue(0, 0, 1)
   228→
   229→    @staticmethod
   230→    def reduce_xor(a: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   231→        mask = (1 << a.width) - 1
   232→        if a.known_mask != mask:
   233→            return TriValue(0, 0, 1)
   234→        return TriValue(bin(a.value_mask & mask).count('1') % 2, 1, 1)
   235→
   236→
   237→OPERATOR_ARITY = {
   238→    'NOT': (1, 1), 'BUF': (1, 1),
   239→    'AND': (2, None), 'OR': (2, None), 'XOR': (2, None),
   240→    'NAND': (2, None), 'NOR': (2, None), 'XNOR': (2, None),
   241→    'MUX': (3, 3), 'ITE': (3, 3),
   242→    'REDUCE_AND': (1, 1), 'REDUCE_OR': (1, 1), 'REDUCE_XOR': (1, 1),
   243→    'EQ': (2, 2),
   244→}
   245→
   246→_UNARY_PROPAGATE = frozenset({'NOT', 'BUF'})
   247→_BINARY_PROPAGATE = frozenset({'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'})
   248→_SELECT = frozenset({'MUX', 'ITE'})
   249→_REDUCE = frozenset({'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR'})
   250→
   251→_BINARY_OPS = {
   252→    'AND': TriValue.and2, 'NAND': TriValue.and2,
   253→    'OR': TriValue.or2, 'NOR': TriValue.or2,
   254→    'XOR': TriValue.xor2, 'XNOR': TriValue.xor2,
   255→}
   256→_NEGATED = frozenset({'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'})
   257→
   258→
   259→def _all_signal_widths(circuit) -> dict:
   260→    return {sig.name: sig.width for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
   261→
   262→
   263→def _expr_width(expr, signal_widths: dict) -> int:
   264→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   265→        return expr.width
   266→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   267→        return signal_widths[expr.name]
   268→    if isinstance(expr, Index):
   269→        return 1
   270→    if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   271→        return expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   272→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   273→        return sum(_expr_width(p, signal_widths) for p in expr.parts)
   274→    if isinstance(expr, Call):
   275→        op = expr.operator
   276→        if op in _UNARY_PROPAGATE | _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
   277→            return _expr_width(expr.args[0], signal_widths)
   278→        if op in _SELECT:
   279→            return _expr_width(expr.args[1], signal_widths)
   280→        if op in _REDUCE | {'EQ'}:
   281→            return 1
   282→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   283→    raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   284→
   285→
   286→def _collect_dependencies(expr) -> set:
   287→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   288→        return {expr.name}
   289→    if isinstance(expr, (Index, Slice)):
   290→        return _collect_dependencies(expr.expr)
   291→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   292→        deps = set()
   293→        for part in expr.parts:
   294→            deps.update(_collect_dependencies(part))
   295→        return deps
   296→    if isinstance(expr, Call):
   297→        deps = set()
   298→        for arg in expr.args:
   299→            deps.update(_collect_dependencies(arg))
   300→        return deps
   301→    return set()
   302→
   303→
   304→def parse_literal(text: str, filename: str = None, line: int = None, col: int = None) -> Tuple[int, int]:
   305→    original = text
   306→    text = text.replace('_', '')
   307→
   308→    if text == 'X':
   309→        raise CircError("CircParseError", "Literal 'X' values are not allowed in .circ files", filename, line, col)
   310→
   311→    if text in ('0', '1'):
   312→        return int(text), 1
   313→
   314→    if text.lower().startswith('0b'):
   315→        digits = text[2:]
   316→        if not digits:
   317→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid binary literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   318→        if not re.match(r'^[01]+$', digits):
   319→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid binary literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   320→        return int(digits, 2), len(digits)
   321→
   322→    if text.lower().startswith('0x'):
   323→        digits = text[2:]
   324→        if not digits:
   325→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid hex literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   326→        if not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   327→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid hex literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   328→        value = int(digits, 16)
   329→        return value, len(digits) * 4
   330→
   331→    sized_match = re.match(r"^(\d+)'([bhd])([0-9a-fA-F_]+)$", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   332→    if sized_match:
   333→        width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   334→        base = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   335→        digits = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   336→
   337→        if width <= 0:
   338→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal width: {original}", filename, line, col)
   339→
   340→        base_validators = {
   341→            'b': (r'^[01]+$', 2, f"Invalid binary literal: {original}"),
   342→            'h': (r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', 16, f"Invalid hex literal: {original}"),
   343→            'd': (r'^[0-9]+$', 10, f"Invalid decimal literal: {original}"),
   344→        }
   345→        pattern, base_int, err_msg = base_validators[base]
   346→        if not re.match(pattern, digits):
   347→            raise CircError("CircParseError", err_msg, filename, line, col)
   348→        try:
   349→            value = int(digits, base_int)
   350→        except ValueError:
   351→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   352→
   353→        if value > (1 << width) - 1:
   354→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Sized decimal value {value} exceeds width {width}", filename, line, col)
   355→        return value, width
   356→
   357→    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   358→
   359→
   360→def parse_3val_input(value_str: str, expected_width: int, signal_name: str, filename: str = None) -> TriValue:
   361→    original = value_str
   362→    text = value_str.replace('_', '')
   363→
   364→    if text in ('0', '1'):
   365→        return TriValue.from_int(int(text), expected_width)
   366→    if text.upper() == 'X':
   367→        return TriValue(0, 0, expected_width)
   368→
   369→    if text.lower().startswith('0b'):
   370→        digits = text[2:]
   371→        if not digits or not re.match(r'^[01Xx]+$', digits):
   372→            raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   373→        if len(digits) != expected_width:
   374→            raise EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",
   375→                           f"Input width mismatch for '{signal_name}': expected width {expected_width}, got width {len(digits)}")
   376→        return _parse_binary_with_x(digits, expected_width)
   377→
   378→    if text.lower().startswith('0x'):
   379→        digits = text[2:]
   380→        if not digits or 'x' in digits.lower() or not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   381→            raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   382→        width = len(digits) * 4
   383→        if width != expected_width:
   384→            raise EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",
   385→                           f"Input width mismatch for '{signal_name}': expected width {expected_width}, got width {width}")
   386→        return TriValue.from_int(int(digits, 16), expected_width)
   387→
   388→    sized_match = re.match(r"^(\d+)'([bhd])([0-9a-fA-FxX_]+)$", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   389→    if sized_match:
   390→        width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   391→        base = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   392→        digits = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   393→
   394→        if width <= 0:
   395→            raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   396→        if width != expected_width:
   397→            raise EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",
   398→                           f"Input width mismatch for '{signal_name}': expected width {expected_width}, got width {width}")
   399→
   400→        if base == 'b':
   401→            if not re.match(r'^[01Xx]+$', digits):
   402→                raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   403→            return _parse_binary_with_x(digits, width)
   404→
   405→        if 'x' in digits.lower():
   406→            raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   407→
   408→        if base == 'h':
   409→            if not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   410→                raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   411→            value = int(digits, 16)
   412→        else:
   413→            if not re.match(r'^[0-9]+$', digits):
   414→                raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   415→            try:
   416→                value = int(digits, 10)
   417→            except ValueError:
   418→                raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   419→
   420→        if value > (1 << width) - 1:
   421→            raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   422→        return TriValue.from_int(value, width)
   423→
   424→    raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   425→
   426→
   427→def _parse_binary_with_x(digits: str, width: int) -> TriValue:
   428→    value_mask = 0
   429→    known_mask = 0
   430→    for i, digit in enumerate(digits):
   431→        bit_pos = width - 1 - i
   432→        if digit.upper() != 'X':
   433→            known_mask |= (1 << bit_pos)
   434→            if digit == '1':
   435→                value_mask |= (1 << bit_pos)
   436→    return TriValue(value_mask, known_mask, width)
   437→
   438→
   439→def format_trivalue(value: TriValue) -> str:
   440→    if value.width == 1:
   441→        if not (value.known_mask & 1):
   442→            return 'X'
   443→        return '1' if (value.value_mask & 1) else '0'
   444→    return '0b' + ''.join(value.get_bit(i) for i in range(value.width - 1, -1, -1))
   445→
   446→
   447→def format_value(value: int, width: int, radix: str) -> str:
   448→    if width == 1:
   449→        return str(value & 1)
   450→    if radix == 'bin':
   451→        return f"0b{bin(value)[2:].zfill(width)}"
   452→    if radix == 'hex':
   453→        hex_width = (width + 3) // 4
   454→        return f"0x{hex(value)[2:].zfill(hex_width)}"
   455→    return str(value)
   456→
   457→
   458→def _split_comma_separated(text: str) -> list:
   459→    parts = []
   460→    current = ""
   461→    brace_depth = 0
   462→    paren_depth = 0
   463→    for ch in text:
   464→        if ch == '{':
   465→            brace_depth += 1
   466→            current += ch
   467→        elif ch == '}':
   468→            brace_depth -= 1
   469→            current += ch
   470→        elif ch == '(':
   471→            paren_depth += 1
   472→            current += ch
   473→        elif ch == ')':
   474→            paren_depth -= 1
   475→            current += ch
   476→        elif ch == ',' and brace_depth == 0 and paren_depth == 0:
   477→            parts.append(current.strip())
   478→            current = ""
   479→        else:
   480→            current += ch
   481→    if current.strip():
   482→        parts.append(current.strip())
   483→    return parts
   484→
   485→
   486→class Parser:
   487→    def __init__(self, content: str, filename: str):
   488→        self.filename = filename
   489→        self.lines = content.split('\n')
   490→
   491→    def parse(self) -> 'Circuit':
   492→        circuit = Circuit()
   493→        in_declarations = True
   494→        for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(self.lines, 1):
   495→            stripped = raw_line.strip()
   496→            if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   497→                continue
   498→            if self._is_declaration(stripped):
   499→                if not in_declarations:
   500→                    raise CircError("DeclarationAfterAssignmentError", "Declaration after assignment is not allowed",
   501→                                   self.filename, line_num, 1)
   502→                self._parse_declaration(stripped, circuit, line_num)
   503→            elif '=' in stripped:
   504→                in_declarations = False
   505→                self._parse_assignment(stripped, circuit, line_num, raw_line)
   506→            else:
   507→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {stripped}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   508→        return circuit
   509→
   510→    def _is_declaration(self, line: str) -> bool:
   511→        parts = line.split()
   512→        return '=' not in line and bool(parts) and parts[0].upper() in ('INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE')
   513→
   514→    def _parse_declaration(self, line: str, circuit: 'Circuit', line_num: int):
   515→        parts = line.split()
   516→        if not parts:
   517→            return
   518→        keyword = parts[0].upper()
   519→        target_map = {'INPUT': circuit.inputs, 'OUTPUT': circuit.outputs, 'WIRE': circuit.wires}
   520→        if keyword not in target_map:
   521→            return
   522→        target = target_map[keyword]
   523→        existing_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   524→        for part in parts[1:]:
   525→            vec_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\[(\d+):(\d+)\]$', part)
   526→            if vec_match:
   527→                name, msb, lsb = vec_match.group(1), int(vec_match.group(2)), int(vec_match.group(3))
   528→                if msb < lsb:
   529→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: msb ({msb}) must be >= lsb ({lsb})",
   530→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   531→                if lsb < 0:
   532→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: lsb ({lsb}) must be >= 0",
   533→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   534→                if name in existing_names:
   535→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}",
   536→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   537→                target.append(Signal(name, msb, lsb))
   538→                existing_names.add(name)
   539→            else:
   540→                if not self._is_valid_identifier(part):
   541→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid identifier: {part}",
   542→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   543→                if part in existing_names:
   544→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {part}",
   545→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   546→                target.append(Signal(part, 0, 0))
   547→                existing_names.add(part)
   548→
   549→    @staticmethod
   550→    def _is_valid_identifier(name: str) -> bool:
   551→        return bool(name) and not name[0].isdigit() and all(c.isalnum() or c == '_' for c in name)
   552→
   553→    def _parse_assignment(self, line: str, circuit: 'Circuit', line_num: int, raw_line: str):
   554→        eq_pos = line.find('=')
   555→        lhs = line[:eq_pos].strip()
   556→        rhs = line[eq_pos + 1:].strip()
   557→        if not lhs:
   558→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Missing left-hand side in assignment", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   559→        if not re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)$', lhs):
   560→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid left-hand side: {lhs}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   561→        rhs_expr, _ = self._parse_expression(rhs, line_num, eq_pos + 2)
   562→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, raw_line.find(lhs) + 1))
   563→
   564→    def _parse_expression(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   565→        text = text.strip()
   566→        if not text:
   567→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   568→        if text.startswith('{'):
   569→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   570→        expr, consumed = self._parse_primary_expr(text, line_num, start_col)
   571→        remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   572→        while remaining.startswith('['):
   573→            bracket_end = remaining.find(']')
   574→            if bracket_end == -1:
   575→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched bracket in index/slice", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   576→            bracket_content = remaining[1:bracket_end]
   577→            if ':' in bracket_content:
   578→                parts = bracket_content.split(':')
   579→                if len(parts) != 2:
   580→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   581→                try:
   582→                    hi, lo = int(parts[0].strip()), int(parts[1].strip())
   583→                except ValueError:
   584→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice indices: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   585→                expr = Slice(expr, hi, lo)
   586→            else:
   587→                try:
   588→                    idx = int(bracket_content.strip())
   589→                except ValueError:
   590→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid index: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   591→                expr = Index(expr, idx)
   592→            consumed += bracket_end + 1
   593→            remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   594→        return expr, consumed
   595→
   596→    def _try_match_literal(self, text: str):
   597→        m = re.match(r"^(\d+'[bhd])", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   598→        if m:
   599→            prefix = m.group(1).lower()
   600→            rest = text[len(m.group(1)):]
   601→            if prefix.endswith('b'):
   602→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([01_]+)', rest)
   603→            elif prefix.endswith('h'):
   604→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   605→            else:
   606→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9_]+)', rest)
   607→            if digits_m:
   608→                full = m.group(1) + digits_m.group(1)
   609→                end_pos = len(full)
   610→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   611→                    return full
   612→            return None
   613→        if text.lower().startswith('0b') and len(text) > 2:
   614→            m = re.match(r'^(0b[01_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   615→            if m:
   616→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   617→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   618→                    return m.group(1)
   619→            return None
   620→        if text.lower().startswith('0x') and len(text) > 2:
   621→            m = re.match(r'^(0x[0-9a-fA-F_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   622→            if m:
   623→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   624→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   625→                    return m.group(1)
   626→            return None
   627→        if text and text[0] in ('0', '1'):
   628→            if len(text) == 1 or not text[1].isalnum() and text[1] != '_':
   629→                return text[0]
   630→        return None
   631→
   632→    def _parse_primary_expr(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   633→        text = text.strip()
   634→        if not text:
   635→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   636→        if text.startswith('{'):
   637→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   638→        if text.startswith('('):
   639→            depth, i = 1, 1
   640→            while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   641→                if text[i] == '(':
   642→                    depth += 1
   643→                elif text[i] == ')':
   644→                    depth -= 1
   645→                i += 1
   646→            if depth != 0:
   647→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   648→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(text[1:i-1], line_num, start_col + 1)
   649→            return expr, i
   650→        lit_text = self._try_match_literal(text)
   651→        if lit_text is not None:
   652→            value, width = parse_literal(lit_text, self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   653→            return Literal(value, width), len(lit_text)
   654→        call_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\(', text)
   655→        if call_match:
   656→            op_name = call_match.group(1).upper()
   657→            op_start = call_match.end()
   658→            paren_count, i = 1, op_start
   659→            while i < len(text) and paren_count > 0:
   660→                if text[i] == '(':
   661→                    paren_count += 1
   662→                elif text[i] == ')':
   663→                    paren_count -= 1
   664→                i += 1
   665→            if paren_count != 0:
   666→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis in expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   667→            args = self._parse_arguments(text[op_start:i-1], line_num, start_col + op_start)
   668→            return Call(op_name, args), i
   669→        id_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)', text)
   670→        if id_match:
   671→            name = id_match.group(1)
   672→            return Identifier(name), len(name)
   673→        raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid expression: {text[:50]}", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   674→
   675→    def _parse_concatenation(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[Concat, int]:
   676→        if not text.startswith('{'):
   677→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Expected '{' for concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   678→        depth, i = 1, 1
   679→        while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   680→            if text[i] == '{':
   681→                depth += 1
   682→            elif text[i] == '}':
   683→                depth -= 1
   684→            i += 1
   685→        if depth != 0:
   686→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched brace in concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   687→        inner = text[1:i-1]
   688→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(inner)
   689→        if not raw_parts:
   690→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   691→        parts = [self._parse_expression(p, line_num, start_col + 1 + idx)[0] for idx, p in enumerate(raw_parts)]
   692→        return Concat(parts), i
   693→
   694→    def _parse_arguments(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> list:
   695→        text = text.strip()
   696→        if not text:
   697→            return []
   698→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(text)
   699→        args = []
   700→        offset = 0
   701→        for raw in raw_parts:
   702→            idx = text.index(raw, offset)
   703→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(raw, line_num, start_col + idx)
   704→            args.append(expr)
   705→            offset = idx + len(raw)
   706→        return args
   707→
   708→
   709→@dataclass
   710→class Assignment:
   711→    lhs: str
   712→    rhs: object
   713→    line: int
   714→    col: int
   715→
   716→
   717→@dataclass
   718→class Circuit:
   719→    inputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   720→    outputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   721→    wires: list = field(default_factory=list)
   722→    assignments: list = field(default_factory=list)
   723→
   724→    def get_all_names(self) -> set:
   725→        return {sig.name for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires}
   726→
   727→    def get_signal(self, name: str):
   728→        for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires:
   729→            if sig.name == name:
   730→                return sig
   731→        return None
   732→
   733→
   734→class Validator:
   735→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit, filename: str):
   736→        self.circuit = circuit
   737→        self.filename = filename
   738→        self.signal_types = {sig.name: 'input' for sig in circuit.inputs}
   739→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'output' for sig in circuit.outputs})
   740→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'wire' for sig in circuit.wires})
   741→        self.signal_info = {sig.name: sig for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
   742→        self._all_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   743→        self._signal_widths = {name: sig.width for name, sig in self.signal_info.items()}
   744→
   745→    def validate(self):
   746→        self._check_assignments()
   747→        self._check_cycles()
   748→
   749→    def _check_assignments(self):
   750→        assigned = set()
   751→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   752→            if asn.lhs not in self._all_names:
   753→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   754→            if self.signal_types.get(asn.lhs) == 'input':
   755→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   756→            if asn.lhs in assigned:
   757→                raise CircError("MultipleAssignmentError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   758→            assigned.add(asn.lhs)
   759→            self._validate_expr(asn.rhs, asn.line)
   760→        for sig in self.circuit.outputs + self.circuit.wires:
   761→            if sig.name not in assigned:
   762→                kind = "Output" if self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output' else "Wire"
   763→                raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", self.filename)
   764→
   765→    def _validate_expr(self, expr, line_num: int) -> int:
   766→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   767→            return expr.width
   768→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   769→            if expr.name not in self._all_names:
   770→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", self.filename, line_num)
   771→            return self._signal_widths[expr.name]
   772→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
   773→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   774→            self._check_index_bounds(expr, line_num)
   775→            return 1
   776→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   777→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   778→            if expr.hi < expr.lo:
   779→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: hi ({expr.hi}) < lo ({expr.lo})",
   780→                               self.filename, line_num)
   781→            self._check_slice_bounds(expr, line_num)
   782→            return expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   783→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   784→            return sum(self._validate_expr(p, line_num) for p in expr.parts)
   785→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   786→            self._check_operator_arity(expr, line_num)
   787→            return self._check_operator_width(expr, line_num)
   788→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   789→
   790→    def _check_index_bounds(self, expr: Index, line_num: int):
   791→        sig = self.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
   792→        if sig:
   793→            if expr.index < sig.lsb or expr.index > sig.msb:
   794→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   795→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
   796→                               self.filename, line_num)
   797→        else:
   798→            base_width = self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   799→            if expr.index < 0 or expr.index >= base_width:
   800→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   801→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
   802→                               self.filename, line_num)
   803→
   804→    def _check_slice_bounds(self, expr: Slice, line_num: int):
   805→        sig = self.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
   806→        if sig:
   807→            if expr.lo < sig.lsb or expr.hi > sig.msb:
   808→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   809→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
   810→                               self.filename, line_num)
   811→        else:
   812→            base_width = self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   813→            if expr.lo < 0 or expr.hi >= base_width:
   814→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   815→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
   816→                               self.filename, line_num)
   817→
   818→    def _check_operator_arity(self, call: Call, line_num: int):
   819→        op = call.operator
   820→        num_args = len(call.args)
   821→        if op not in OPERATOR_ARITY:
   822→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Unknown operator: {op}", self.filename, line_num)
   823→        min_args, max_args = OPERATOR_ARITY[op]
   824→        if num_args < min_args or (max_args and num_args > max_args):
   825→            if max_args == min_args:
   826→                req = f"exactly {min_args}"
   827→            elif max_args is None:
   828→                req = f"at least {min_args}"
   829→            else:
   830→                req = f"between {min_args} and {max_args}"
   831→            plural = "s" if min_args > 1 or (max_args and max_args > 1) else ""
   832→            raise CircError("ArityError", f"Operator {op} requires {req} argument{plural}, got {num_args}", self.filename, line_num)
   833→
   834→    def _check_operator_width(self, call: Call, line_num: int) -> int:
   835→        op = call.operator
   836→        arg_widths = [self._validate_expr(arg, line_num) for arg in call.args]
   837→        if op in _UNARY_PROPAGATE:
   838→            return arg_widths[0]
   839→        if op in _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
   840→            for i, w in enumerate(arg_widths[1:], 1):
   841→                if w != arg_widths[0]:
   842→                    raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   843→                                   f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand {i} has width {w}",
   844→                                   self.filename, line_num)
   845→            return arg_widths[0]
   846→        if op in _SELECT:
   847→            if arg_widths[0] != 1:
   848→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   849→                               f"Selector in {op} must have width 1, got width {arg_widths[0]}", self.filename, line_num)
   850→            if arg_widths[1] != arg_widths[2]:
   851→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   852→                               f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}, operand 2 has width {arg_widths[2]}",
   853→                               self.filename, line_num)
   854→            return arg_widths[1]
   855→        if op in _REDUCE:
   856→            return 1
   857→        if op == 'EQ':
   858→            if arg_widths[0] != arg_widths[1]:
   859→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   860→                               f"Width mismatch in EQ: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}",
   861→                               self.filename, line_num)
   862→            return 1
   863→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   864→
   865→    def _check_cycles(self):
   866→        dependencies = {sig.name: set() for sig in self.circuit.inputs}
   867→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   868→            dependencies[asn.lhs] = _collect_dependencies(asn.rhs)
   869→        WHITE, GRAY, BLACK = 0, 1, 2
   870→        color = {name: WHITE for name in self._all_names}
   871→
   872→        def dfs(node: str, path: list):
   873→            color[node] = GRAY
   874→            path.append(node)
   875→            for dep in dependencies.get(node, set()):
   876→                if color[dep] == GRAY:
   877→                    cycle_start = path.index(dep)
   878→                    return path[cycle_start:] + [dep]
   879→                if color[dep] == WHITE:
   880→                    result = dfs(dep, path)
   881→                    if result:
   882→                        return result
   883→            path.pop()
   884→            color[node] = BLACK
   885→            return None
   886→
   887→        for node in self._all_names:
   888→            if color[node] == WHITE:
   889→                cycle = dfs(node, [])
   890→                if cycle:
   891→                    raise CircError("CycleError", f"Cycle detected: {' -> '.join(cycle)}", self.filename)
   892→
   893→
   894→class Evaluator:
   895→    """Evaluates a circuit using 3-valued logic. For 2-valued mode, inputs are
   896→    fully-known TriValues and results are converted to int at output time."""
   897→
   898→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit):
   899→        self.circuit = circuit
   900→        self._signal_widths = _all_signal_widths(circuit)
   901→
   902→    def evaluate(self, inputs: dict) -> dict:
   903→        self.values = dict(inputs)
   904→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   905→            self.values[asn.lhs] = self._eval_expr(asn.rhs)
   906→        return {sig.name: self.values[sig.name] for sig in self.circuit.outputs}
   907→
   908→    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> TriValue:
   909→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   910→            return TriValue.from_int(expr.value, expr.width)
   911→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   912→            return self.values[expr.name]
   913→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
   914→            val = self._eval_expr(expr.expr)
   915→            return TriValue(
   916→                (val.value_mask >> expr.index) & 1,
   917→                (val.known_mask >> expr.index) & 1,
   918→                1
   919→            )
   920→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   921→            val = self._eval_expr(expr.expr)
   922→            width = expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   923→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
   924→            return TriValue(
   925→                (val.value_mask >> expr.lo) & mask,
   926→                (val.known_mask >> expr.lo) & mask,
   927→                width
   928→            )
   929→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   930→            result_value = 0
   931→            result_known = 0
   932→            total_width = 0
   933→            for part in expr.parts:
   934→                w = _expr_width(part, self._signal_widths)
   935→                part_val = self._eval_expr(part)
   936→                result_value = (result_value << w) | (part_val.value_mask & ((1 << w) - 1))
   937→                result_known = (result_known << w) | (part_val.known_mask & ((1 << w) - 1))
   938→                total_width += w
   939→            return TriValue(result_value, result_known, total_width)
   940→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   941→            args = [self._eval_expr(arg) for arg in expr.args]
   942→            return self._apply_operator(expr.operator, args)
   943→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   944→
   945→    def _apply_operator(self, op: str, args: list) -> TriValue:
   946→        if op == 'NOT':
   947→            return TriValue.not_(args[0])
   948→        if op == 'BUF':
   949→            return args[0].copy()
   950→
   951→        if op in _BINARY_OPS:
   952→            result = args[0]
   953→            for a in args[1:]:
   954→                result = _BINARY_OPS[op](result, a)
   955→            if op in _NEGATED:
   956→                result = TriValue.not_(result)
   957→            return result
   958→
   959→        if op in _SELECT:
   960→            return TriValue.mux(args[0], args[1], args[2])
   961→
   962→        if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
   963→            return TriValue.reduce_and(args[0])
   964→        if op == 'REDUCE_OR':
   965→            return TriValue.reduce_or(args[0])
   966→        if op == 'REDUCE_XOR':
   967→            return TriValue.reduce_xor(args[0])
   968→        if op == 'EQ':
   969→            return TriValue.eq(args[0], args[1])
   970→
   971→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   972→
   973→
   974→def output_json(data: dict):
   975→    print(json.dumps(data, separators=(',', ':')))
   976→
   977→
   978→def load_circuit(filename: str, command: str, json_mode: bool):
   979→    try:
   980→        with open(filename, 'r') as f:
   981→            content = f.read()
   982→    except (FileNotFoundError, IOError):
   983→        return output_error(CircError("FileNotFoundError", f"Cannot read file: {filename}", filename), command, json_mode)
   984→    try:
   985→        circuit = Parser(content, filename).parse()
   986→        Validator(circuit, filename).validate()
   987→    except CircError as e:
   988→        return output_error(e, command, json_mode)
   989→    return circuit
   990→
   991→
   992→def signal_list(signals):
   993→    return [{"name": s.name, "msb": s.msb, "lsb": s.lsb} for s in sorted(signals, key=lambda s: s.name)]
   994→
   995→
   996→def get_exit_code(error_type: str) -> int:
   997→    if error_type in VALIDATION_ERRORS:
   998→        return EXIT_VALIDATION_ERROR
   999→    return ERROR_EXIT_CODES.get(error_type, 1)
  1000→
  1001→
  1002→def output_error(error, command: str, json_mode: bool) -> int:
  1003→    exit_code = get_exit_code(error.error_type)
  1004→    if json_mode:
  1005→        output_json({"ok": False, "command": command, "exit_code": exit_code, "error": error.to_dict()})
  1006→    else:
  1007→        loc = ""
  1008→        if hasattr(error, 'file') and error.file:
  1009→            loc = f"{error.file}:"
  1010→            if error.line:
  1011→                loc += f"{error.line}:"
  1012→                if error.col:
  1013→                    loc += f"{error.col}:"
  1014→            loc += " "
  1015→        print(f"{loc}Error: {error.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1016→    return exit_code
  1017→
  1018→
  1019→def check_command(args: list, json_mode: bool) -> int:
  1020→    if not args:
  1021→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
  1022→    filename = args[0]
  1023→    result = load_circuit(filename, "check", json_mode)
  1024→    if isinstance(result, int):
  1025→        return result
  1026→    circuit = result
  1027→    inputs = signal_list(circuit.inputs)
  1028→    outputs = signal_list(circuit.outputs)
  1029→    if json_mode:
  1030→        output_json({"ok": True, "command": "check", "format": "circ", "inputs": inputs, "outputs": outputs})
  1031→    else:
  1032→        print("Circuit is valid.")
  1033→        print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in inputs)}")
  1034→        print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in outputs)}")
  1035→    return 0
  1036→
  1037→
  1038→def _parse_eval_args(args, json_mode):
  1039→    """Parse eval command arguments. Returns (opts_dict, error_result_or_None)."""
  1040→    if not args:
  1041→        return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
  1042→    opts = {
  1043→        'filename': args[0],
  1044→        'set_values': {},
  1045→        'default_value': None,
  1046→        'allow_extra': False,
  1047→        'radix': 'bin',
  1048→        'mode': '2val',
  1049→    }
  1050→    i = 1
  1051→    while i < len(args):
  1052→        arg = args[i]
  1053→        if arg == '--set':
  1054→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1055→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--set requires a name=value argument"), "eval", json_mode)
  1056→            set_arg = args[i + 1]
  1057→            if '=' not in set_arg:
  1058→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --set format: {set_arg}, expected name=value"), "eval", json_mode)
  1059→            name, value = set_arg.split('=', 1)
  1060→            opts['set_values'][name] = value
  1061→            i += 2
  1062→        elif arg == '--default':
  1063→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1064→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--default requires a value (0 or 1)"), "eval", json_mode)
  1065→            default_arg = args[i + 1]
  1066→            if default_arg not in ('0', '1'):
  1067→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --default value: {default_arg}, must be 0 or 1"), "eval", json_mode)
  1068→            opts['default_value'] = int(default_arg)
  1069→            i += 2
  1070→        elif arg == '--allow-extra':
  1071→            opts['allow_extra'] = True
  1072→            i += 1
  1073→        elif arg == '--radix':
  1074→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1075→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--radix requires a value (bin, hex, or dec)"), "eval", json_mode)
  1076→            radix_arg = args[i + 1].lower()
  1077→            if radix_arg not in ('bin', 'hex', 'dec'):
  1078→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --radix value: {radix_arg}, must be bin, hex, or dec"), "eval", json_mode)
  1079→            opts['radix'] = radix_arg
  1080→            i += 2
  1081→        elif arg == '--mode':
  1082→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1083→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--mode requires a value (2val or 3val)"), "eval", json_mode)
  1084→            mode_arg = args[i + 1].lower()
  1085→            if mode_arg not in ('2val', '3val'):
  1086→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --mode value: {mode_arg}, must be 2val or 3val"), "eval", json_mode)
  1087→            opts['mode'] = mode_arg
  1088→            i += 2
  1089→        elif '=' in arg:
  1090→            name, value = arg.split('=', 1)
  1091→            opts['set_values'][name] = value
  1092→            i += 1
  1093→        else:
  1094→            return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Unknown option: {arg}"), "eval", json_mode)
  1095→    return opts, None
  1096→
  1097→
  1098→def _default_fill(default_value, width):
  1099→    """Compute the fill value for a signal when using --default."""
  1100→    if width == 1:
  1101→        return TriValue.from_int(default_value, 1)
  1102→    fill = default_value * ((1 << width) - 1) if default_value else 0
  1103→    return TriValue.from_int(fill, width)
  1104→
  1105→
  1106→def eval_command(args: list, json_mode: bool) -> int:
  1107→    opts, err = _parse_eval_args(args, json_mode)
  1108→    if err is not None:
  1109→        return err
  1110→
  1111→    filename = opts['filename']
  1112→    set_values = opts['set_values']
  1113→    default_value = opts['default_value']
  1114→    allow_extra = opts['allow_extra']
  1115→    radix = opts['radix']
  1116→    mode = opts['mode']
  1117→
  1118→    if mode == '3val' and radix != 'bin':
  1119→        return output_error(EvalError("RadixNotAllowedIn3ValError", "Only --radix bin is allowed with --mode 3val"), "eval", json_mode)
  1120→
  1121→    result = load_circuit(filename, "eval", json_mode)
  1122→    if isinstance(result, int):
  1123→        return result
  1124→    circuit = result
  1125→    input_names = {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}
  1126→
  1127→    for name in set_values:
  1128→        if name not in input_names and not allow_extra:
  1129→            return output_error(EvalError("UnknownInputError", f"Unknown input: {name}"), "eval", json_mode)
  1130→
  1131→    missing_inputs = input_names - set(set_values.keys())
  1132→    if missing_inputs and default_value is None:
  1133→        return output_error(EvalError("MissingInputError", f"Missing input values for: {', '.join(sorted(missing_inputs))}"), "eval", json_mode)
  1134→
  1135→    # Build TriValue inputs for all modes
  1136→    inputs = {}
  1137→    try:
  1138→        for sig in circuit.inputs:
  1139→            if sig.name in set_values:
  1140→                inputs[sig.name] = parse_3val_input(set_values[sig.name], sig.width, sig.name, filename)
  1141→            elif default_value is not None:
  1142→                inputs[sig.name] = _default_fill(default_value, sig.width)
  1143→    except EvalError as e:
  1144→        return output_error(e, "eval", json_mode)
  1145→
  1146→    try:
  1147→        results = Evaluator(circuit).evaluate(inputs)
  1148→    except Exception as e:

  1150→
  1151→    # Format outputs
  1152→    sorted_outputs = sorted(results.items(), key=lambda x: x[0])
  1153→
  1154→    if mode == '2val':
  1155→        if json_mode:
  1156→            output_list = []
  1157→            for name, val in sorted_outputs:
  1158→                sig = circuit.get_signal(name)
  1159→                w = sig.width if sig else 1
  1160→                output_list.append({
  1161→                    "name": name,
  1162→                    "msb": sig.msb if sig else 0,
  1163→                    "lsb": sig.lsb if sig else 0,
  1164→                    "value": format_value(val.to_int(), w, radix)
  1165→                })
  1166→            output_json({
  1167→                "ok": True, "command": "eval", "mode": "2val", "radix": radix,
  1168→                "inputs": signal_list(circuit.inputs),
  1169→                "outputs": output_list
  1170→            })
  1171→        else:
  1172→            for name, val in sorted_outputs:
  1173→                sig = circuit.get_signal(name)
  1174→                w = sig.width if sig else 1
  1175→                print(f"{name}={format_value(val.to_int(), w, radix)}")
  1176→    else:
  1177→        if json_mode:
  1178→            output_list = []
  1179→            for name, val in sorted_outputs:
  1180→                sig = circuit.get_signal(name)
  1181→                output_list.append({

  1184→                    "lsb": sig.lsb if sig else 0,

  1187→            output_json({

  1189→                "inputs": signal_list(circuit.inputs),

  1192→        else:

  1199→def main():
  1200→    args = sys.argv[1:]
  1201→    if '--help' in args:
  1202→        print("""Usage: circopt.py [OPTIONS] <COMMAND> [ARGS]
  1203→
  1204→Options:
  1205→  --help     Show this help message and exit
  1206→  --version  Show version and exit
  1207→  --json     Output in JSON format
  1208→
  1209→Commands:
  1210→  check      Validate a .circ circuit file
  1211→  eval       Evaluate a circuit with given inputs
  1212→
  1213→Run 'circopt.py <COMMAND> --help' for more information on a command.""")
  1214→        return 0
  1215→    if '--version' in args:
  1216→        json_mode = '--json' in args
  1217→        if json_mode:
  1218→            output_json({"ok": True, "command": "__version__", "version": VERSION})
  1219→        else:
  1220→            print(VERSION)

  1222→    json_mode = '--json' in args
  1223→    args = [a for a in args if a != '--json']
  1224→    if not args:
  1225→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "No command provided. Use --help for usage information."), "__cli__", json_mode)
  1226→    command, *command_args = args

content.thinking:
Now I have a complete understanding of the existing codebase. Let me plan the implementation for Part 5:

1. **New error types**:
   - `UnknownInputFormatError` - exit 1
   - `JsonParseError` - exit 2
   - `JsonSchemaError` - exit 3
   - `BenchParseError` - exit 2
   - `RedefinitionError` - exit 3

2. **New global flag `--format`**:
   - Accepts: `auto`, `circ`, `json`, `bench`
   - Default is `auto`
   - Auto-detection by extension: `.circ` → circ, `.json` → json, `.bench` → bench
   - Unknown extension → exit 1, `UnknownInputFormatError`

3. **JSON circuit format**:
   - Schema with `format_version`, `inputs`, `outputs`, `wires`, `assignments`
   - Port objects: `{"name": "<identifier>", "msb": <int>, "lsb": <int>}`
   - Assignment objects: `{"lhs": "<identifier>", "rhs": "<expression string>"}`
   - Expression parsing reuses existing expression grammar
   - `format_version` must be exactly 1
   - Every wire and output must appear exactly once as assignment lhs

4. **BENCH circuit format**:
   - Comments start with `#`
   - `INPUT(name)` - declare input
   - `OUTPUT(name)` - declare output reference
   - `lhs = OP(arg1, arg2, ...)` - wire assignment
   - Supported operators: NOT, BUF (arity 1), AND, OR, XOR, NAND, NOR, XNOR (arity ≥ 2)
   - Scalar-only: no `[` or `]` in identifiers
   - No literals in RHS
   - No duplicate LHS (RedefinitionError)
   - OUTPUT references must exist (input or defined wire)
   - Wires implicitly declared by assignment

I'll proceed with implementing the necessary modifications. The check output will now include a "format" field with possible values of "circ", "json", or "bench". This requires careful integration of the new error types and parsing logic to ensure robust handling of different circuit representations.

   706→            idx = text.index(raw, offset)

   712→
   713→def parse_json_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   714→    """Parse a JSON circuit file."""
   715→    try:
   716→        data = json.loads(content)
   717→    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   718→        raise CircError("JsonParseError", f"Invalid JSON: {e.msg}", filename, e.lineno, e.colno)
   719→
   720→    # Validate schema
   721→    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   722→        raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", "JSON root must be an object", filename)
   723→
   724→    if "format_version" not in data:
   725→        raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", "Missing required field: format_version", filename)
   726→
   727→    if data["format_version"] != 1:
   728→        raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Unsupported format_version: {data.get('format_version')}, expected 1", filename)
   729→
   730→    for field in ("inputs", "outputs", "wires", "assignments"):
   731→        if field not in data:
   732→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Missing required field: {field}", filename)
   733→        if not isinstance(data[field], list):
   734→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Field '{field}' must be an array", filename)
   735→
   736→    circuit = Circuit()
   737→    all_names = set()
   738→
   739→    # Parse ports
   740→    def parse_port(port_data, target_list, kind):
   741→        if not isinstance(port_data, dict):
   742→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} must be an object", filename)
   743→        if "name" not in port_data:
   744→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} missing required field: name", filename)
   745→        name = port_data["name"]
   746→        if not isinstance(name, str):
   747→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} name must be a string", filename)
   748→        if not re.match(r'^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$', name):
   749→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Invalid identifier: {name}", filename)
   750→
   751→        msb = port_data.get("msb", 0)
   752→        lsb = port_data.get("lsb", 0)
   753→        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):
   754→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} msb and lsb must be integers", filename)
   755→        if msb < lsb:
   756→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} msb ({msb}) must be >= lsb ({lsb})", filename)
   757→        if lsb < 0:
   758→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} lsb ({lsb}) must be >= 0", filename)
   759→
   760→        if name in all_names:
   761→            raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}", filename)
   762→        all_names.add(name)
   763→        target_list.append(Signal(name, msb, lsb))
   764→
   765→    for port in data["inputs"]:
   766→        parse_port(port, circuit.inputs, "Input port")
   767→    for port in data["outputs"]:
   768→        parse_port(port, circuit.outputs, "Output port")
   769→    for port in data["wires"]:
   770→        parse_port(port, circuit.wires, "Wire port")
   771→
   772→    # Parse assignments
   773→    assigned_lhs = set()
   774→    for i, asn_data in enumerate(data["assignments"]):
   775→        if not isinstance(asn_data, dict):
   776→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} must be an object", filename)
   777→        if "lhs" not in asn_data:
   778→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} missing required field: lhs", filename)
   779→        if "rhs" not in asn_data:
   780→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} missing required field: rhs", filename)
   781→
   782→        lhs = asn_data["lhs"]
   783→        rhs_str = asn_data["rhs"]
   784→
   785→        if not isinstance(lhs, str):
   786→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} lhs must be a string", filename)
   787→        if not isinstance(rhs_str, str):
   788→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} rhs must be a string", filename)
   789→
   790→        # Check that lhs is declared
   791→        if lhs not in all_names:
   792→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {lhs}", filename)
   793→
   794→        # Check that every output and wire appears exactly once as LHS
   795→        if lhs in assigned_lhs:
   796→            raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {lhs}", filename)
   797→        assigned_lhs.add(lhs)
   798→
   799→        # Parse RHS expression using existing parser
   800→        parser = Parser("", filename)
   801→        try:
   802→            rhs_expr, _ = parser._parse_expression(rhs_str, 1, 1)
   803→        except CircError as e:
   804→            raise CircError(e.error_type, e.message, filename, 1, e.col)
   805→
   806→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, 1, 1))
   807→
   808→    # Check that all outputs and wires are assigned
   809→    for sig in circuit.outputs + circuit.wires:
   810→        if sig.name not in assigned_lhs:
   811→            kind = "Output" if sig in circuit.outputs else "Wire"
   812→            raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", filename)
   813→
   814→    return circuit
   815→
   816→
   817→def parse_bench_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   818→    """Parse a BENCH circuit file."""
   819→    circuit = Circuit()
   820→    all_names = set()
   821→    defined_wires = set()  # Wires defined by assignment
   822→    output_refs = set()  # OUTPUT() references
   823→    assignments_map = {}  # lhs -> (rhs_str, line_num)
   824→
   825→    lines = content.split('\n')
   826→
   827→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   828→        # Remove comments
   829→        if '#' in line:
   830→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
   831→        line = line.strip()
   832→        if not line:
   833→            continue
   834→
   835→        # Check for brackets in the line (not allowed in BENCH)
   836→        if '[' in line or ']' in line:
   837→            raise CircError("BenchParseError", "BENCH format does not support vector signals (brackets not allowed)", filename, line_num)
   838→
   839→        # Parse INPUT(name)
   840→        input_match = re.match(r'^INPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   841→        if input_match:
   842→            name = input_match.group(1)
   843→            if name in all_names:
   844→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal defined multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   845→            all_names.add(name)
   846→            circuit.inputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   847→            continue
   848→
   849→        # Parse OUTPUT(name)
   850→        output_match = re.match(r'^OUTPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   851→        if output_match:
   852→            name = output_match.group(1)
   853→            if name in output_refs:
   854→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"OUTPUT declared multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   855→            output_refs.add(name)
   856→            circuit.outputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   857→            continue
   858→
   859→        # Parse assignment: lhs = OP(args)
   860→        assign_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.+)$', line)
   861→        if assign_match:
   862→            lhs = assign_match.group(1)
   863→            rhs_str = assign_match.group(2).strip()
   864→
   865→            if lhs in defined_wires:
   866→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   867→
   868→            # Check if lhs is an input (cannot assign to inputs)
   869→            if lhs in all_names and lhs not in output_refs:
   870→                # It's already defined as input
   871→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   872→
   873→            # Add to defined wires and all_names
   874→            defined_wires.add(lhs)
   875→            all_names.add(lhs)
   876→
   877→            # Store assignment for later processing
   878→            assignments_map[lhs] = (rhs_str, line_num)
   879→            continue
   880→
   881→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {line}", filename, line_num)
   882→
   883→    # Add wire declarations for defined wires (excluding outputs that reference inputs)
   884→    wire_names = defined_wires - output_refs
   885→    for name in wire_names:
   886→        circuit.wires.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   887→
   888→    # Now parse all assignments
   889→    parser = Parser("", filename)
   890→    for lhs, (rhs_str, line_num) in assignments_map.items():
   891→        # Parse RHS - it must be a function call in BENCH format
   892→        try:
   893→            rhs_expr, _ = parser._parse_expression(rhs_str, line_num, 1)
   894→        except CircError as e:
   895→            raise CircError(e.error_type, e.message, filename, line_num, e.col)
   896→
   897→        # Validate BENCH-specific constraints
   898→        _validate_bench_expr(rhs_expr, filename, line_num, all_names)
   899→
   900→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, 1))
   901→
   902→    # Validate OUTPUT references - must reference either an input or a defined wire
   903→    for output_sig in circuit.outputs:
   904→        name = output_sig.name
   905→        if name not in all_names:
   906→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {name}", filename)
   907→        # If it's not an input and not defined by assignment, it should be a wire reference
   908→        # We need to ensure the output is actually driven
   909→        if name not in defined_wires and name not in {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}:
   910→            # Output references an undefined wire
   911→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {name}", filename)
   912→
   913→    return circuit
   914→
   915→
   916→def _validate_bench_expr(expr, filename: str, line_num: int, defined_names: set):
   917→    """Validate that a BENCH expression contains no literals and uses defined names."""
   918→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   919→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Literals are not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)

  1126→class Evaluator:

  1140→    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> TriValue:
  1141→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1142→            return TriValue.from_int(expr.value, expr.width)
  1143→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
  1144→            return self.values[expr.name]
  1145→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
  1146→            val = self._eval_expr(expr.expr)
  1147→            return TriValue(
  1148→                (val.value_mask >> expr.index) & 1,
  1149→                (val.known_mask >> expr.index) & 1,
  1150→                1
  1151→            )
  1152→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
  1153→            val = self._eval_expr(expr.expr)
  1154→            width = expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
  1155→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
  1156→            return TriValue(
  1157→                (val.value_mask >> expr.lo) & mask,
  1158→                (val.known_mask >> expr.lo) & mask,
  1159→                width
  1160→            )
  1161→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1162→            result_value = 0
  1163→            result_known = 0
  1164→            total_width = 0
  1165→            for part in expr.parts:
  1166→                w = _expr_width(part, self._signal_widths)
  1167→                part_val = self._eval_expr(part)
  1168→                result_value = (result_value << w) | (part_val.value_mask & ((1 << w) - 1))
  1169→                result_known = (result_known << w) | (part_val.known_mask & ((1 << w) - 1))
  1170→                total_width += w
  1171→            return TriValue(result_value, result_known, total_width)
  1172→        if isinstance(expr, Call):

   714→    """Parse a JSON circuit file."""

   717→    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:

   816→
   817→def parse_bench_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   818→    """Parse a BENCH circuit file."""
   819→    circuit = Circuit()
   820→    all_names = set()
   821→    defined_wires = set()  # Wires defined by assignment
   822→    output_refs = set()  # OUTPUT() references
   823→    assignments_map = {}  # lhs -> (rhs_str, line_num)
   824→
   825→    lines = content.split('\n')
   826→
   827→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   828→        # Remove comments
   829→        if '#' in line:
   830→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
   831→        line = line.strip()
   832→        if not line:
   833→            continue
   834→
   835→        # Check for brackets in the line (not allowed in BENCH)
   836→        if '[' in line or ']' in line:
   837→            raise CircError("BenchParseError", "BENCH format does not support vector signals (brackets not allowed)", filename, line_num)
   838→
   839→        # Parse INPUT(name)
   840→        input_match = re.match(r'^INPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   841→        if input_match:
   842→            name = input_match.group(1)
   843→            if name in all_names:
   844→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal defined multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   845→            all_names.add(name)
   846→            circuit.inputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   847→            continue
   848→
   849→        # Parse OUTPUT(name)
   850→        output_match = re.match(r'^OUTPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   851→        if output_match:
   852→            name = output_match.group(1)
   853→            if name in output_refs:
   854→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"OUTPUT declared multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   855→            output_refs.add(name)
   856→            circuit.outputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   857→            continue
   858→
   859→        # Parse assignment: lhs = OP(args)
   860→        assign_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.+)$', line)
   861→        if assign_match:
   862→            lhs = assign_match.group(1)
   863→            rhs_str = assign_match.group(2).strip()
   864→
   865→            if lhs in defined_wires:
   866→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   867→
   868→            # Check if lhs is an input (cannot assign to inputs)
   869→            if lhs in all_names and lhs not in output_refs:
   870→                # It's already defined as input
   871→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   872→
   873→            # Add to defined wires and all_names
   874→            defined_wires.add(lhs)
   875→            all_names.add(lhs)
   876→
   877→            # Store assignment for later processing
   878→            assignments_map[lhs] = (rhs_str, line_num)
   879→            continue
   880→
   881→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {line}", filename, line_num)
   882→
   883→    # Add wire declarations for defined wires (excluding outputs that reference inputs)
   884→    wire_names = defined_wires - output_refs
   885→    for name in wire_names:
   886→        circuit.wires.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   887→
   888→    # Now parse all assignments
   889→    parser = Parser("", filename)
   890→    for lhs, (rhs_str, line_num) in assignments_map.items():
   891→        # Parse RHS - it must be a function call in BENCH format
   892→        try:
   893→            rhs_expr, _ = parser._parse_expression(rhs_str, line_num, 1)
   894→        except CircError as e:
   895→            raise CircError(e.error_type, e.message, filename, line_num, e.col)
   896→
   897→        # Validate BENCH-specific constraints
   898→        _validate_bench_expr(rhs_expr, filename, line_num, all_names)
   899→
   900→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, 1))
   901→
   902→    # Validate OUTPUT references - must reference either an input or a defined wire
   903→    for output_sig in circuit.outputs:
   904→        name = output_sig.name
   905→        if name not in all_names:
   906→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {name}", filename)
   907→        # If it's not an input and not defined by assignment, it should be a wire reference
   908→        # We need to ensure the output is actually driven
   909→        if name not in defined_wires and name not in {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}:
   910→            # Output references an undefined wire
   911→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {name}", filename)
   912→
   913→    return circuit
   914→
   915→
   916→def _validate_bench_expr(expr, filename: str, line_num: int, defined_names: set):
   917→    """Validate that a BENCH expression contains no literals and uses defined names."""
   918→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   919→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Literals are not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   920→
   921→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   922→        if expr.name not in defined_names:
   923→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", filename, line_num)

   719→
   720→
   721→@dataclass
   722→class Circuit:
   723→    inputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   724→    outputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   725→    wires: list = field(default_factory=list)
   726→    assignments: list = field(default_factory=list)
   727→
   728→    def get_all_names(self) -> set:
   729→        return {sig.name for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires}
   730→
   731→    def get_signal(self, name: str):
   732→        for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires:
   733→            if sig.name == name:
   734→                return sig
   735→        return None
   736→
   737→
   738→class Validator:
   739→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit, filename: str):
   740→        self.circuit = circuit
   741→        self.filename = filename
   742→        self.signal_types = {sig.name: 'input' for sig in circuit.inputs}
   743→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'output' for sig in circuit.outputs})
   744→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'wire' for sig in circuit.wires})
   745→        self.signal_info = {sig.name: sig for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
   746→        self._all_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   747→        self._signal_widths = {name: sig.width for name, sig in self.signal_info.items()}
   748→
   749→    def validate(self):
   750→        self._check_assignments()
   751→        self._check_cycles()
   752→
   753→    def _check_assignments(self):
   754→        assigned = set()
   755→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   756→            if asn.lhs not in self._all_names:
   757→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   758→            if self.signal_types.get(asn.lhs) == 'input':
   759→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   760→            if asn.lhs in assigned:
   761→                raise CircError("MultipleAssignmentError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   762→            assigned.add(asn.lhs)
   763→            self._validate_expr(asn.rhs, asn.line)
   764→        for sig in self.circuit.outputs + self.circuit.wires:
   765→            if sig.name not in assigned:
   766→                kind = "Output" if self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output' else "Wire"
   767→                raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", self.filename)
   768→
   769→    def _validate_expr(self, expr, line_num: int) -> int:
   770→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   771→            return expr.width
   772→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   773→            if expr.name not in self._all_names:
   774→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", self.filename, line_num)
   775→            return self._signal_widths[expr.name]
   776→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
   777→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)

   738→def parse_json_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   739→    """Parse a JSON circuit file."""
   740→    try:

   768→        if "name" not in port_data:

   778→        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):

   780→        if msb < lsb:

   782→        if lsb < 0:

   842→def parse_bench_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   843→    """Parse a BENCH circuit file."""
   844→    circuit = Circuit()
   845→    all_names = set()
   846→    defined_wires = set()  # Wires defined by assignment
   847→    output_refs = set()  # OUTPUT() references
   848→    assignments_list = []  # [(lhs, rhs_str, line_num)]
   849→
   850→    lines = content.split('\n')
   851→
   852→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   853→        # Remove comments
   854→        if '#' in line:
   855→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
   856→        line = line.strip()
   857→        if not line:
   858→            continue
   859→
   860→        # Check for brackets in the line (not allowed in BENCH)
   861→        if '[' in line or ']' in line:
   862→            raise CircError("BenchParseError", "BENCH format does not support vector signals (brackets not allowed)", filename, line_num)
   863→
   864→        # Parse INPUT(name)
   865→        input_match = re.match(r'^INPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   866→        if input_match:
   867→            name = input_match.group(1)
   868→            if name in all_names:
   869→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal defined multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   870→            all_names.add(name)
   871→            circuit.inputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   872→            continue
   873→
   874→        # Parse OUTPUT(name)
   875→        output_match = re.match(r'^OUTPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   876→        if output_match:
   877→            name = output_match.group(1)
   878→            if name in output_refs:
   879→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"OUTPUT declared multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   880→            output_refs.add(name)
   881→            circuit.outputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   882→            continue
   883→
   884→        # Parse assignment: lhs = OP(args)
   885→        assign_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.+)$', line)
   886→        if assign_match:
   887→            lhs = assign_match.group(1)
   888→            rhs_str = assign_match.group(2).strip()
   889→
   890→            if lhs in defined_wires:
   891→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   892→
   893→            # Check if lhs is an input (cannot assign to inputs)
   894→            if lhs in all_names and lhs not in output_refs:
   895→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   896→
   897→            # Add to defined wires and all_names
   898→            defined_wires.add(lhs)
   899→            all_names.add(lhs)
   900→
   901→            # Store assignment for later processing
   902→            assignments_list.append((lhs, rhs_str, line_num))
   903→            continue

   906→
   907→    # Add wire declarations for defined wires (excluding outputs that reference inputs or other wires)
   908→    wire_names = defined_wires - output_refs
   909→    for name in wire_names:
   910→        circuit.wires.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   911→
   912→    # Now parse all assignments
   913→    parser = Parser("", filename)
   914→    for lhs, rhs_str, line_num in assignments_list:
   915→        # Parse RHS - it must be a function call in BENCH format
   916→        try:
   917→            rhs_expr, _ = parser._parse_expression(rhs_str, line_num, 1)
   918→        except CircError as e:
   919→            raise CircError(e.error_type, e.message, filename, line_num, e.col)
   920→
   921→        # Validate BENCH-specific constraints

   924→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, 1))

   926→    # Validate OUTPUT references - must reference either an input or a defined wire

   929→        if name not in all_names:
   930→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {name}", filename)
   931→        if name not in defined_wires and name not in {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}:
   932→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {name}", filename)
   933→
   934→    return circuit

   937→def _validate_bench_expr(expr, filename: str, line_num: int, defined_names: set):
   938→    """Validate that a BENCH expression contains no literals and uses defined names."""
   939→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):

   944→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", filename, line_num)
